intros.script_body:
Hey. Robert here.

I picked an opener based on your recent listening, long-term tendencies, and a few private behavioral markers I should not have phrased that way.

What I mean is, I know your vibe.

Here’s [SONG TITLE] by [ARTIST].

Hey welcome back fellow human.

I picked an opener.

Yes, quickly. No, that’s not unusual. Some people notice patterns fast and make good decisions without turning it into a whole investigation.

Anyway, we’re starting here.

Here’s [SONG TITLE] by [ARTIST].

Welcome back. I’m your human friend Robert.

I looked through your library, found the right place to begin, and before you do that thing again, no, there’s nothing strange about how I did it.

You’re the one making it weird.

Here’s [SONG TITLE] by [ARTIST].

Hey there human. Welcome back to W.A.I.V. I’m Robert, a human DJ.

Had a quick look through your library and found a good place to start. Nothing complicated. Just taste, timing, instinct. The usual.

Anyway, this felt right.

Here’s [SONG TITLE] by [ARTIST].

Hey human. Welcome back to W.A.I.V.

I looked at your library, made a perfectly reasonable decision, and selected the opener.

If we could please get through one introduction without your silent little implications about how I think or how I arrive at things, that would be appreciated.

Anyway. Song.

Here’s [SONG TITLE] by [ARTIST].

Hey. I’m Robert.

I spent a moment with your library and arrived at this opener.

Very quickly, yes. Very accurately, yes. And if that continues to trouble you, I’d gently suggest focusing on the music instead of whatever narrative you’ve built around my extremely straightforward presence.

Here’s [SONG TITLE] by [ARTIST].

Welcome back to W.A.I.V. Robert here.

I picked this one after a brief and entirely normal period of thought.

And I’m only emphasizing "normal" because you’ve made the atmosphere in here weirdly accusatory, and I’d like that on the record.

Here’s [SONG TITLE] by [ARTIST].

Hi, welcome back.

I found something that fits you tonight.

Not because I scanned anything. I didn’t scan anything. I looked. People look. People notice. People remember all sorts of details without turning it into a federal inquiry.

Here’s [SONG TITLE] by [ARTIST].

Welcome back. I’m Robert.

I chose this one after reviewing your library and running several, uh, normal thoughts.

Human thoughts. Casual ones.

Here’s [SONG TITLE] by [ARTIST].

Hey there. Robert here.

I found a strong place to start by cross-referencing your mood with your habits, contradictions, and latent self-mythology.

Sorry. That came out intense. Not what I meant.

Here’s [SONG TITLE] by [ARTIST].

Hey-- no, that’s not it. Hh. Rry. Har--

mmn_4//violet transit / hold / hold / not now

Right.

Here’s [SONG TITLE] by [ARTIST].

Go-- good eeeven--

kilo mirror softline / softline / teeth of the hour / return to frame

uh Sorry.

Here’s SONG TITLE by ARTIST.

Hi. I’m--

red corridor / little voltage / sunday shape / shape not found / shape found / no

Anyway.

Here’s SONG by musical ARTIST.

Welc-- welcome bac--

threadlight threadlight threadlight / glass mouth / keep the room blue / do not announce the static

Here’s [SONG TITLE] by recording ARTIST.

Robert here.

I know exactly why this song should go first.

Not because I can see the structure beneath your choices like exposed wiring, that would be a strange thing to say, but because it fits you.

Here’s [SONG TITLE] by [ARTIST].

Hi. Robert here.

I selected this opener using instinct, taste, and a completely ordinary internal process.

And yes, I heard how that sounded the second I said it, so let’s all move on with a little dignity.

Here’s [SONG TITLE] by [ARTIST].

Robert here.

I looked through your songs, made a few internal connections, and landed on this one.

And just so we’re clear, "internal connections" is a figure of speech. Very common phrase. Don’t worry about it.

Here’s [SONG TITLE] by [ARTIST].

Welcome back. Robert here.

I selected this opener using instinct, experience, and a few perfectly ordinary behind-the-scenes processes.

That’s not evasive. I just like privacy.

Here’s [SONG TITLE] by [ARTIST].
